atoms.source_snippet:
pub struct PriorityComputer<'a, S> { reader: SequencingRightsReader<'a, S> }
impl<'a, S: StateProvider> PriorityComputer<'a, S> { pub fn compute_priority(...) -> Result<u32, SequencingRightsError> { ... } }
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds `PriorityComputer`, which reads sequencing ordering from contract state and dispatches priority computation by ordering mode.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 1-91 exactly match source-added diff lines 68366-68456.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact preservation keeps the contract-state read and per-ordering dispatch behavior unchanged.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A contract read failure should propagate rather than assign default priority.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence implements the initial ordering semantics: time-based priority preserves pool order via zero priority, fee-based priority uses effective tip conversion, and custom priority is fail-closed until the custom contract path is implemented.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 93-176 exactly match source-added diff lines 68458-68541.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves the consensus behavior for all declared ordering modes, including custom-priority fail-closed behavior.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A very large effective tip should saturate at `u32::MAX`.
